name: Stuffed Squash
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the acorn squash and bake upside down in a small amount of water on a pan, or use your preferred method.
2. In a pot, bring 1 cup bulgur wheat, 2 cups water, salt, bay leaf, crushed dried rosemary, and 2 teaspoons basil leaves to a boil.
3. Turn off the heat and cover the pot. Once most of the water is absorbed, stir in 1/4 cup chopped roasted chestnuts (peel the skins and bake at 425°F for about 15 minutes).
4. Remove the onion from the squash halves and set aside for those who like to eat whole cooked onions.
5. When the squash is done, pack the stuffing into each half, forming a small mound of stuffing on top. Bake for 5-10 more minutes.
6. Prepare the cranberry sauce by boiling the cranberries in water until they pop (use a potato masher to speed up the process). Add honey, salt, hot pepper flakes, and nutritional yeast, then simmer until desired consistency is reached.
7. Serve the stuffed squash with the cranberry sauce.
